feed.title: Computer & Robot Vision
feed.subtitle: Official Website for IEEE RAS Technical Commitee for Computer & Robot Vision

entry.title: Top 3 Advancements in Computer & Robot Vision@2024

link.title: Top 3 Advancements in Computer & Robot Vision@2024

entry.content:
In recent years, the field of computer & robot vision has seen several significant advancements. Here are the top three:

  1. Deep Learning for Semantic Understanding: Deep learning has revolutionized the ability of computers and robots to understand and interpret visual data at a semantic level. Leveraging large datasets and the computational power of GPUs, deep learning models, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformer-based architectures, have achieved state-of-the-art performance in various vision tasks.

  2. Factor Graph Optimization for Visual-Inertial Odometry and SLAM: Factor graph optimization has become a cornerstone in the development of robust visual-inertial odometry (VIO) and simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) systems. This approach models the problem as a graph where nodes represent states (e.g., robot poses) and edges represent constraints (e.g., measurements). Factor graph optimizaition has significantly advanced probabilistic inference, optimization on manifolds with application to VIO and SLAM.

  3. Advanced Map Representations: The development of advanced map representations has greatly enhanced the ability of robots to understand and navigate complex environments. These representations, including voxel grids, meshes and scene graphs, enable efficient storage, manipulation, and querying of spatial information. Emerging techniques such as Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) and Gaussian splatting represent scenes with continuous volumetric fields or point-based representations that encode spatial and appearance information. These methods enable high-quality 3D reconstructions and novel view synthesis, pushing the boundaries of visual understanding and rendering.

These advancements have propelled the capabilities of computer & robot vision, enabling more sophisticated and practical applications across different sectors.
entry.summary: In recent years, the field of computer & robot vision has seen several significant advancements. Here are the top three:

entry.title: In-Person Mentoring Lunch@ICRA 2024
entry.content: Our TC has organized an in-person mentoring lunch with our senior TC members at ICRA 2024. Our new student representatives also had lunch together, sharing ideas about computer & robot vision. Welcome on board!
entry.summary: Our TC has organized an in-person mentoring lunch with our senior TC members at ICRA 2024. Our new student representatives also had lunch together, sharing ideas about computer & robot vision. Welcome on board!

entry.title: Virtual Mentoring Event@ICRA 2024
entry.content:
The virtual mentoring event about faculty applications in robotics organized by our TC has been successfully held on May 2, 2024, 11:00am-12:30pm ET. You can find our slides below.

Luca Carlone: How to Apply for Your Dream Academic Job
Margarita Chli: Faculty Positions in Robotics – reflections from own experience
Chen Wang: Lessons I have learned in the Faculty Job Market
Lu Gan: Faculty Job Application Experience Sharing

We look forward to meeting you at out in-person mentoring lunch during ICRA24!
entry.summary: The virtual mentoring event about faculty applications in robotics organized by our TC has been successfully held on May 2, 2024, 11:00am-12:30pm ET. You can find our slides below.

entry.title: IROS’23 2nd Advanced Marine Robotics TC Workshop

link.title: IROS’23 2nd Advanced Marine Robotics TC Workshop

entry.content: The IROS 2023 2nd Advanced Marine Robotics TC Workshop: Towards Long-term Marine Robotics was held on Oct 1. Sponsored by our TC, selected best papers received $100 monetary prizes.

entry.title: IROS’23 Workshop on Differentiable Probabilistic Robotics

link.title: IROS’23 Workshop on Differentiable Probabilistic Robotics

entry.content: The IROS 2023 Workshop on Differentiable Probabilistic Robotics: Emerging Perspectives on Robot Learning was held on Oct 1 with over 200 registrants and great participation. Sponsored by our TC, two selected best papers received $100 monetary prizes.

entry.title: IROS’23 Workshop on Robotic Perception and Mapping

link.title: IROS’23 Workshop on Robotic Perception and Mapping

entry.content:
The IROS 2023 Workshop on Robotic Perception and Mapping: Frontier Vision & Learning Techniques was held on Oct 5 with 388 registrants. Video recording is now available at the workshop website and added below. Sponsored by our TC, selected best papers received $100 monetary prizes.

Video recording: https://youtu.be/c1ov1rWNgEc.

entry.title: Workshops@IROS 2023
entry.content:
Multiple workshops we endorsed have been accepted at IROS2023:

10/05/23 IROS2023 Robotic Perception and Mapping: Emerging Techniques
10/05/23 IROS2023 Learning Robot Super Autonomy
10/01/23 IROS2023 2nd Advanced Marine Robotics TC Workshop: Towards Long-term Marine Robotics
10/01/23 IROS2023 Differentiable Probabilistic Robotics: Emerging Perspectives on Robot Learning

Please contact us if you want to request an endorsement letter from this TC for a workshop proposal. Financial support is also available for selected workshops.

We look forward to supporting many interesting workshops!
entry.summary: Multiple workshops we endorsed have been accepted at IROS2023:

entry.title: Workshops@ICRA 2023
entry.content:
We are endorsing multiple workshops in computer and robot vision at ICRA2023:

06/02/23 ICRA2023 CoPerception: Collaborative Perception and Learning
06/02/23 ICRA2023 Geometric Representations: The Roles of Modern Screw Theory, Lie algebra, and Geometric Algebra in Robotics
06/02/23 ICRA2023 Human-Robot Teaming: From Data To Systems
06/02/23 ICRA2023 TIG-IV: Agri-Food Robotics–From Farm To Fork
06/02/23 ICRA2023 Computer Vision for Wearable Robotics
05/29/23 ICRA2023 3rd Workshop on Representing and Manipulating Deformable Objects

Please contact us if you want to request an endorsement letter from this TC for a workshop proposal. Financial support is also available for selected workshops.

We look forward to supporting many interesting workshops!
entry.summary: We are endorsing multiple workshops in computer and robot vision at ICRA2023:

entry.title: New TC Membership System
entry.content:
The IEEE Robotics and Automation Society is taking steps to incorporate the Technical Committees (TCs) into existing IEEE infrastructure and improve the data privacy options. This will allow for easier management, communication, and organization.

From now on, you can become a member of the IEEE TC on Computer & Robot Vision by going to the IEEE Membership catalog and checking out our TC. All you will need is a free IEEE Account, an IEEE membership is NOT required, and joining our TC is free.

If you are a current member enrolled via previous smart sheet, please re-register to our TC through the new system.
entry.summary: The IEEE Robotics and Automation Society is taking steps to incorporate the Technical Committees (TCs) into existing IEEE infrastructure and improve the data privacy options. This will allow for easier management, communication, and organization.

entry.title: Inclusion@RSS 2023
entry.content:
The IEEE RAS TC on Computer and Robot Vision is sponsoring the Inclusion@RSS 2023 fellowship program to enable and enhance the participation of traditionally underrepresented groups at Robotics: Science and Systems.

Apply before May 15!
entry.summary: The IEEE RAS TC on Computer and Robot Vision is sponsoring the Inclusion@RSS 2023 fellowship program to enable and enhance the participation of traditionally underrepresented groups at Robotics: Science and Systems.
